zh.lifeSummary: Matilda Perry於大約1133年出生在英國英格蘭越可爾。她於1154年與Sir William Le Vavasour在英國英格蘭諾斯 越可爾約克結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1191年6月22日在英國越可爾塔德卡斯特享年59歲。

nameMeanings.name: Perry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Welsh: Anglicized form of Welsh ap Herry ‘son of Herry’, a variant of the personal name Harry (see Harris ).

English: topographic name for someone who lived near a pear tree (Middle English piri(e), peri(e), puri(e), Old English pirige, pyrige), or a habitational name for someone who owned or came from a place so named. This surname and a number of variants have been established in Ireland since the 17th century.

variants.name: Peery
meanings: Irish: variant of   Perry  , taken to Ireland at the time of thePlantation of Ulster and in the Cromwellian period.

variants.name: Pear
meanings: English: topographic name from residence by a pear tree (Middle English pere, Old English peru, pere). Compare Perry .
English: nickname from Middle English pere, Old French per, peer ‘peer or paragon’, also ‘companion’.

variants.name: Peary
meanings: History: Robert E. Peary (1856–1920) was a U.S.-born explorer, who in 1909 led the first successful expedition to the North Pole. Peary Land in the north of Greenland is named after him, as he explored there in 1892 and 1900.

variants.name: Perrie
meanings: Scottish: variant of Perry 2. Alternatively, perhaps from the Old French personal name Pierre; see Pierce .

variants.name: Harris
meanings: English (southern England and south Wales): from the personal name Harry + genitival -s. This surname is also established in Ireland, taken there principally during the Plantation of Ulster. However, in some cases, particularly in families coming from County Mayo, Harris can be an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hEarchadha. This surname is also very common among African Americans.

American shortened and altered form of Greek surnames begining with Cha(r)-, such as Chasandrinos (variant of Kassandrinos, a habitational name from the Kassandra peninsula of Chalkidiki), and various patronymics from the personal name Charalampos (see Charos ). In North America, the surname Harris may possibly also originate from a transferred use of the Greek personal (given) name Charis or Harris (shortened forms of Charalampos) as a surname (i.e. as a replacement of the original surname).
Americanized form of various like-sounding Jewish surnames.

variants.name: Perryman
meanings: English (Devon): elaborated form of Perry , with Middle English man, probably denoting one who lived near a pear tree; see Pearman .

nameMeanings.name: Matilda
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

variants.name: Mathilda
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

en.lifeSummary: Matilda Perry was born about 1133, in Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. She married Sir William Le Vavasour in 1154, in York, North Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 4 daughters. She died on 22 June 1191, in Tadcaster, Yorkshire, England, at the age of 59.

Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée le 22 juin 1191, à Tadcaster, Everwyck, Angleterre, à l’âge de 59 ans.

ko.Hazlewood, Yorkshire, England: 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 하즐루드 비 타드카스터
ko.lifeSummary: Matilda Perry(은)는 대략 1133년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1154년 영국 잉글랜드 노르스 요르크실르 요르크에서 Sir William Le Vavasour(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1191년 6월 22일 영국 요르크실르 타드카스터에서 59세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Matilda Perry родилась около 1133 г. в местности еркшир, Англия, Великобритания. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir William Le Vavasour в 1154 г. в местности ерк, норт еркшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 4 дочери. Она умерла 22 июня 1191 г. в местности тадкастир, еркшир, Англия в возрасте 59 лет.
